title: Application Menus and Modules interview basics
question: How do you explain Application Menus and Modules in a ServiceNow interview?
summary: Adds direct coverage for the Application Menus and Modules topic so it is no longer a taxonomy-only entry.
exactAnswer: Application menus organize the left navigation into logical application areas, while modules are the individual navigator entries inside those menus. In interviews, explain that modules are just entry points into lists, forms, pages, maps, reports, or scripted destinations, so naming and audience clarity matter.
keyPoints: Describe what the feature controls.
Explain when you would configure it instead of scripting around it.
Mention one governance or supportability consideration.
examples: Use Application Menus and Modules deliberately in a governed implementation instead of treating it as a one-off configuration.

title: Choice Lists interview basics
question: What should you know about Choice Lists?
summary: Adds direct coverage for the Choice Lists topic so it is no longer a taxonomy-only entry.
exactAnswer: Choice lists define controlled values for a field so data entry stays consistent and reporting stays reliable. A strong interview answer mentions that choices can be dictionary-based, localized, environment-specific, and should be governed so teams do not create inconsistent state or category values over time.
keyPoints: Explain why it exists in the data model.
Mention one operational benefit.
Mention one common mistake or support consideration.
examples: Use Choice Lists deliberately in a governed implementation instead of treating it as a one-off configuration.

title: Display Values interview basics
question: What should you know about Display Values?
summary: Adds direct coverage for the Display Values topic so it is no longer a taxonomy-only entry.
exactAnswer: Display values are the user-friendly values shown for reference or choice fields, while the platform often stores sys_ids or internal values underneath. In interviews, explain that confusion between stored values and display values is a common cause of integration and scripting bugs.
keyPoints: Explain why it exists in the data model.
Mention one operational benefit.
Mention one common mistake or support consideration.
examples: Use Display Values deliberately in a governed implementation instead of treating it as a one-off configuration.

title: Domain Separation interview basics
question: How do you explain Domain Separation from a platform security perspective?
summary: Adds direct coverage for the Domain Separation topic so it is no longer a taxonomy-only entry.
exactAnswer: Domain separation partitions data and configuration so multiple business units or customers can operate on one instance with controlled visibility. A strong answer should mention data isolation, scoped administration, and the additional complexity it adds to design and support.
keyPoints: Connect it to least privilege or safe operations.
Mention why support teams use it carefully.
Explain one common misuse to avoid.
examples: Use Domain Separation deliberately in a governed implementation instead of treating it as a one-off configuration.

title: GraphQL vs REST Discussion interview basics
question: How do you explain GraphQL vs REST Discussion in an integration interview?
summary: Adds direct coverage for the GraphQL vs REST Discussion topic so it is no longer a taxonomy-only entry.
exactAnswer: REST is generally the default integration style in ServiceNow because it is broadly supported and maps well to platform APIs, while GraphQL is more selective and request-shape driven. In interviews, explain that the choice depends on the provider system, data retrieval needs, governance, and support maturity.
keyPoints: Mention transport or payload behavior.
Explain how you would test it safely.
Mention error handling or observability.
examples: Use GraphQL vs REST Discussion deliberately in a governed implementation instead of treating it as a one-off configuration.

title: Impersonation interview basics
question: How do you explain Impersonation from a platform security perspective?
summary: Adds direct coverage for the Impersonation topic so it is no longer a taxonomy-only entry.
exactAnswer: Impersonation lets an administrator temporarily assume another user context to validate access, visibility, and end-user behavior. It is powerful for testing, but it should be used carefully and auditable because it changes what the platform shows and allows during that session.
keyPoints: Connect it to least privilege or safe operations.
Mention why support teams use it carefully.
Explain one common misuse to avoid.
examples: Use Impersonation deliberately in a governed implementation instead of treating it as a one-off configuration.

title: Jelly interview basics
question: How do you explain Jelly in a ServiceNow interview?
summary: Adds direct coverage for the Jelly topic so it is no longer a taxonomy-only entry.
exactAnswer: Jelly is the XML-based templating technology used in older ServiceNow UI components such as UI Pages and certain legacy interfaces. In interviews, frame Jelly as legacy platform knowledge that still matters for support and upgrades even though newer UI patterns are preferred for new work.
keyPoints: Explain the business or technical purpose.
Mention where it is configured or observed.
Give one practical implementation consideration.
examples: Use Jelly deliberately in a governed implementation instead of treating it as a one-off configuration.

summary: Adds direct coverage for the Mobile Actions topic so it is no longer a taxonomy-only entry.

keyPoints: Explain the business or technical purpose.

title: Upgrades interview basics
question: How do you explain Upgrades in a ServiceNow interview?
summary: Adds direct coverage for the Upgrades topic so it is no longer a taxonomy-only entry.
exactAnswer: Upgrades should be explained as a governance and validation process, not only as a technical version jump. A strong answer mentions skipped changes, regression testing, deprecated behavior review, and stakeholder communication.
keyPoints: Explain the business or technical purpose.
Mention where it is configured or observed.
Give one practical implementation consideration.
examples: Use Upgrades deliberately in a governed implementation instead of treating it as a one-off configuration.

title: Variable Sets interview basics
question: How do you explain Variable Sets in a ServiceNow interview?
summary: Adds direct coverage for the Variable Sets topic so it is no longer a taxonomy-only entry.
exactAnswer: Variable sets group reusable catalog variables that can be attached to multiple items. They improve consistency and reduce duplication when several catalog items need the same requester inputs.
keyPoints: Explain the business or technical purpose.
Mention where it is configured or observed.
Give one practical implementation consideration.
examples: Use Variable Sets deliberately in a governed implementation instead of treating it as a one-off configuration.

summary: Adds direct coverage for the Virtual Agent Topics topic so it is no longer a taxonomy-only entry.
